headline: University Research Funding Drops to 0.4% of GDP
context: Malaysia's gross expenditure on R&D in higher education fell to 0.4% of GDP in 2025, down from 0.5% in 2020.

cards.big: Research funding at 0.4% of GDP puts Malaysia behind Vietnam at 0.5% in 2025
cards.sub: South Korea invests 2.1% and Singapore invests 1.8% of GDP in R&D

cards.big: Public universities produced only 3,200 patent filings in 2025 across all fields
cards.sub: Commercialization rate of university patents stands at just 4.8%

cards.big: FRGS grant funding has been frozen at RM300 million annually since 2019
cards.sub: Inflation means real research purchasing power dropped 18% over 6 years

cards.big: Top 5 Malaysian universities lost 340 senior researchers to foreign institutions since 2022
cards.sub: Better funding and academic freedom cited as primary pull factors

cards.big: A nation that underfunds research imports solutions instead of creating them
Should Malaysia mandate that 1% of GDP goes to public research funding
